Title: Spatial consistency of cell growth direction during organ morphogenesis requires CELLULOSE SYNTHASE INTERACTIVE1

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase interactive protein 1; microtubule-associated cellulose synthesis guidance factor
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane/cell cortex associated with cortical microtubules and cellulose synthase complexes
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Main load-bearing component of the plant cell wall that restrains and orients growth during organ morphogenesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall, especially outer epidermal wall of sepals
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function analyzed in developing and mature sepals, especially abaxial epidermis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro live imaging of dissected inflorescences; osmotic treatment with 0.4 M NaCl for plasmolysis/shrinkage assays
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis (csi1-3, csi1-6, csi1-1); complementation with pCSI1::RFP-CSI1; AFM of cellulose microfibrils; Raman microspectroscopy; TIRF colocalization of GFP-CESA3 with mCherry-TUA5; live imaging and growth quantification; mechanical assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSI1 is required for normal sepal elongation, as csi1 sepals are shorter and more curved than wild type. 2. CSI1 loss causes snaky giant cells and reduced spatial consistency of cell growth direction without strongly changing cell growth anisotropy. 3. CSI1 promotes guidance of cellulose synthesis relative to cortical microtubules, affecting cellulose organization across wall thickness and organ mechanical anisotropy.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CSI1 reduces sepal elongation and mechanical anisotropy, disrupts coordination of growth direction, and alters cellulose organization
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced organ elongation; altered cell shape (snaky giant cells); reduced mechanical anisotropy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although newly deposited cellulose microfibrils were locally more aligned in csi1, cellulose organization across the whole wall thickness was less anisotropic, indicating a complex role beyond simple alignment promotion.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct nutrient-engineering value; useful for engineering cell wall architecture and organ-shape traits where cellulose deposition coordination is relevant.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Main load-bearing wall polymer supporting growth control
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant analysis (csi1-3 csi3-1) comparing sepal length and cell snakiness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSI3 alone was reported previously to show no obvious phenotype, but in this study the csi1-3 csi3-1 double mutant had shorter sepals than csi1-3 alone. 2. The double mutant also showed stronger giant-cell snakiness. 3. These data suggest CSI3 partially compensates for CSI1 function during sepal elongation.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partial functional redundancy with CSI1 in cellulose-related control of sepal elongation and cell-shape stability
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced defect when combined with csi1 mutation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CSI3 is supportive rather than the primary focus; included because double-mutant experiments in Results directly tested its contribution.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential modifier gene for cell wall-guidance traits in combination with CSI1.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall load-bearing polymer indirectly patterned by microtubule organization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative mutant phenotyping of ktn1-2 sepals and giant-cell snakiness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ktn1-2 sepals are more rounded than csi1 sepals. 2. Despite reduced elongation, ktn1-2 does not show snaky giant cells and has lower snakiness than wild type. 3. This comparison indicates that reduced elongation and cell snakiness are uncoupled, helping define the specific CSI1 phenotype.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as a comparative control showing that altered microtubule-related growth defects do not necessarily phenocopy CSI1-dependent cell snakiness
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KTN1 is not a primary nutrient-metabolism gene but is experimentally probed in Results as a mechanistic comparator.
